mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative when both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals stronger supply-side capacity utilization, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns despite 3-month rising trends in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures that prompt central banks to raise rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100k indicates robust labor market demand, which drives consumer spending and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns despite PAYEMS growth above 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates stronger consumer demand, which boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns despite 3-month rising trends in RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO) combined with low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates an expansionary monetary policy environment that supports equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: expansionary monetary policy with strong supply growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns when both INDPRO and FEDFUNDS show positive momentum.
